cards.definition: The shade condition — alongside humid, cool weather — that most favors leaf rust disease outbreaks.
cards.analogy: A disease that thrives when the canopy isn't doing its microclimate-buffering job, not one that shade makes worse.
cards.why_it_matters: A reason removing shade for short-term yield can backfire specifically through higher rust pressure, not just lost carbon/moisture benefits.
cards.cloze: Coffee leaf rust disease is favoured by humid, cool weather and ___ overhead shade.

cards.definition: The elevation above which coffee areas are generally spared from leaf rust outbreaks during the February-March risk window.
cards.analogy: A cooler, higher zone the disease's favored conditions simply don't reach at that time of year.
cards.why_it_matters: A plot's elevation is a real, checkable rust-risk indicator — not just a proxy for general climate quality.
cards.cloze: Coffee areas located above ___ feet elevation are generally not affected by leaf rust during Feb-March.

cards.definition: The concentration of Bordeaux mixture applied as a preventive spray against leaf rust, timed before blossom.
cards.analogy: A preventive dose, not a treatment dose — applied before disease pressure arrives, not after.
cards.why_it_matters: Worth confirming this is the pre-blossom spray, since black rot's Bordeaux spray (later in the season) is applied at a different strength.
cards.cloze: The pre-blossom Bordeaux mixture spray used for leaf rust control is applied at ___% concentration.

cards.definition: The concentration of Bordeaux mixture used against black rot, applied at a specific point in early June — a different strength and timing than the leaf-rust spray.
cards.analogy: A stronger, later-season dose than the pre-blossom rust spray — matched to a different disease and a different point in the calendar.
cards.why_it_matters: Confusing this with the pre-blossom leaf-rust spray strength would mean the wrong dose at the wrong time.
cards.cloze: In black rot-affected areas, ___% Bordeaux mixture is sprayed during the first week of June.

cards.definition: The relative humidity range — alongside continuous rain with no dry spell — that most favors black rot outbreaks.
cards.analogy: A disease that needs sustained wetness with no break, not just occasional rain.
cards.why_it_matters: Explains why a run of unbroken monsoon rain is a specific black-rot warning sign, not just generally bad drying weather.
cards.cloze: Black rot disease outbreaks are favoured by continuous rain without a dry spell and relative humidity of ___%.
